proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher numeric `feature_041` is associated with higher `pfs_months`; each 1-unit increase in `feature_041` increases expected progression-free survival.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate linear regression estimated +0.1765 months of `pfs_months` per 1-unit increase in `feature_041` (z=266.53, p<1e-300; r=0.766).

iterations.update: `feature_041` was the dominant positive univariate signal, so later iterations tested whether it persisted after adjustment and whether its slope varied by subgroups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ordinal/numeric `feature_025` is associated with lower `pfs_months`; each 1-unit increase in `feature_025` decreases expected progression-free survival.

analyses.method: univariate linear regression and level means
analyses.result_summary: Univariate regression estimated -1.1897 months per 1-unit increase in `feature_025` (z=-83.99, p<1e-300). Mean `pfs_months` declined across levels: 5.271 at 0, 4.054 at 1, and 2.908 at 2.

iterations.update: `feature_025` showed a large monotone adverse association and was carried into adjusted and interaction models.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_036=1` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_036=0`.

analyses.result_summary: Mean `pfs_months` was 3.703 for `feature_036=1` versus 5.055 for `feature_036=0`; the signed difference was -1.3520 months (z=-68.49, p<1e-300).

iterations.update: `feature_036` was the strongest binary adverse marker and was evaluated as both a main effect and an interaction modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_015=1` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_015=0`.
Patients with `feature_001=1` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_001=0`.

analyses.result_summary: `feature_015=1` was associated with -0.3052 months lower mean `pfs_months` than `feature_015=0` (4.114 vs 4.419; z=-14.84, p<1e-300).
`feature_001=1` was associated with -0.3268 months lower mean `pfs_months` than `feature_001=0` (4.121 vs 4.448; z=-16.14, p<1e-300).

iterations.update: `feature_015` and `feature_001` had moderate adverse main effects and later proved important interaction modifiers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_043=1` have higher mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_043=0`.

analyses.result_summary: Mean `pfs_months` was 5.089 for `feature_043=1` versus 4.117 for `feature_043=0`; the signed difference was +0.9722 months (z=32.02, p<1e-300).

iterations.update: `feature_043` was the clearest favorable binary marker; subsequent iterations tested whether that advantage was consistent across adverse-marker subgroups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher numeric `feature_050` is associated with higher `pfs_months`.
Higher numeric `feature_056` is associated with lower `pfs_months`.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate regression estimated +0.4654 months of `pfs_months` per 1-unit increase in `feature_050` (z=22.87, p<1e-300).
Univariate regression estimated -0.0730 months of `pfs_months` per 1-unit increase in `feature_056` (z=-27.68, p<1e-300).

iterations.update: Both continuous features showed strong main effects and were retained for multivariable adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher numeric `feature_029` is associated with lower `pfs_months`.
Higher numeric `feature_028` is associated with lower `pfs_months`.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate regression estimated -0.00535 months per 1-unit increase in `feature_029` (z=-8.44, p<1e-300).
Univariate regression estimated -0.000317 months per 1-unit increase in `feature_028` (z=-2.50, p=0.0126).

iterations.update: `feature_029` was a small but clear adverse continuous signal; `feature_028` was weaker and needed adjusted testing.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_066=1` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_066=0`.
Patients with `feature_042=1` have higher mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_042=0`.

analyses.result_summary: `feature_066=1` was associated with -0.2279 months lower mean `pfs_months` than `feature_066=0` (z=-5.11, p=3.24e-7).
`feature_042=1` was associated with +0.2211 months higher mean `pfs_months` than `feature_042=0` (z=3.38, p=0.000714).

iterations.update: `feature_066` and `feature_042` were rare binary signals; adjustment was required to distinguish independent effects from confounding.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After simultaneous adjustment for other screened features, higher `feature_041` remains associated with higher `pfs_months`.
After simultaneous adjustment for other screened features, higher `feature_025` remains associated with lower `pfs_months`.
After simultaneous adjustment for other screened features, `feature_036=1` remains associated with lower `pfs_months`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted linear model coefficient for `feature_041` was +0.1758 months per unit (SE=0.000388, z=453.70, p<1e-300).
Adjusted coefficient for `feature_025` was -1.1713 months per unit (SE=0.00569, z=-205.79, p<1e-300).
Adjusted coefficient for `feature_036=1` was -1.3615 months (SE=0.00775, z=-175.61, p<1e-300).

iterations.update: The three strongest main effects were not explained by the other screened covariates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After simultaneous adjustment for other screened features, `feature_043=1` remains associated with higher `pfs_months`.
After simultaneous adjustment for other screened features, higher `feature_050` remains associated with higher `pfs_months`.
After simultaneous adjustment for other screened features, higher `feature_056` remains associated with lower `pfs_months`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for `feature_043=1` was +0.9429 months (SE=0.00964, z=97.86, p<1e-300).
Adjusted coefficient for `feature_050` was +0.4692 months per unit (SE=0.00769, z=61.00, p<1e-300).
Adjusted coefficient for `feature_056` was -0.0761 months per unit (SE=0.00100, z=-76.10, p<1e-300).

iterations.update: The favorable `feature_043` and `feature_050` signals and adverse `feature_056` signal remained strong after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After simultaneous adjustment for other screened features, `feature_015=1` remains associated with lower `pfs_months`.
After simultaneous adjustment for other screened features, `feature_001=1` remains associated with lower `pfs_months`.
After simultaneous adjustment for other screened features, `feature_066=1` remains associated with lower `pfs_months`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for `feature_015=1` was -0.2867 months (SE=0.00807, z=-35.52, p<1e-300).
Adjusted coefficient for `feature_001=1` was -0.3539 months (SE=0.00806, z=-43.90, p<1e-300).
Adjusted coefficient for `feature_066=1` was -0.3565 months (SE=0.01889, z=-18.87, p<1e-300).

iterations.update: These binary adverse features were independent enough to retain adjusted main effects; interaction testing focused on whether they modified favorable markers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After simultaneous adjustment for other screened features, higher `feature_029` remains associated with lower `pfs_months`.
After simultaneous adjustment for other screened features, higher `feature_028` remains associated with lower `pfs_months`.
After simultaneous adjustment for other screened features, `feature_042=1` remains associated with higher `pfs_months`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for `feature_029` was -0.00500 months per unit (SE=0.000238, z=-21.00, p<1e-300).
Adjusted coefficient for `feature_028` was -0.000425 months per unit (SE=0.0000478, z=-8.91, p<1e-300).
Adjusted coefficient for `feature_042=1` was only +0.0208 months and was not significant (SE=0.0229, z=0.91, p=0.362).

iterations.update: `feature_029` and `feature_028` persisted, but the rare positive `feature_042` univariate signal was explained by other covariates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_043=1` and `pfs_months` is attenuated in patients with `feature_001=1`; the `feature_043` by `feature_001` interaction is negative.

analyses.method: linear regression with binary-binary interaction and stratified means
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_043 * feature_001` interaction coefficient was -1.6288 months (z=-32.25, p<1e-300). Stratified differences for `feature_043=1` versus 0 were +1.656 months when `feature_001=0` but only +0.027 months when `feature_001=1`.

iterations.update: `feature_001` nearly eliminated the favorable `feature_043` association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_043=1` and `pfs_months` is attenuated in patients with `feature_015=1`; the `feature_043` by `feature_015` interaction is negative.

analyses.method: linear regression with binary-binary interaction and stratified means
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_043 * feature_015` interaction coefficient was -1.4541 months (z=-27.74, p<1e-300). The `feature_043` difference was +1.480 months when `feature_015=0` and +0.026 months when `feature_015=1`.

iterations.update: `feature_015` also marked a subgroup where the `feature_043` advantage was largely absent.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_043=1` and `pfs_months` is attenuated or reversed in patients with `feature_066=1`; the `feature_043` by `feature_066` interaction is negative.

analyses.method: linear regression with binary-binary interaction and stratified means
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_043 * feature_066` interaction coefficient was -1.1829 months (z=-9.87, p<1e-300). The `feature_043` difference was +1.027 months when `feature_066=0` and -0.156 months when `feature_066=1`.

iterations.update: `feature_066` identified a smaller subgroup where `feature_043` was not favorable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The combined adverse effects of `feature_015=1` and `feature_001=1` are less negative than the sum of their individual effects; the `feature_015` by `feature_001` interaction is positive.

analyses.result_summary: The `feature_015 * feature_001` interaction coefficient was +0.4517 months (z=10.44, p<1e-300), indicating a less-than-additive adverse combination on the `pfs_months` scale.

iterations.update: Adverse binary markers were not purely additive; some combinations showed interaction structure.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association between numeric `feature_029` and `pfs_months` is stronger among patients with `feature_043=1`; the `feature_029` by `feature_043` interaction is negative.

analyses.method: linear regression with continuous-binary interaction and stratified slopes
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_029 * feature_043` interaction coefficient was -0.02527 months per `feature_029` unit (z=-16.04, p<1e-300). The `feature_029` slope was -0.00047 when `feature_043=0` and -0.02574 when `feature_043=1`.

iterations.update: `feature_029` was especially adverse in the otherwise favorable `feature_043=1` subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association between numeric `feature_029` and `pfs_months` is attenuated among patients with `feature_001=1`; the `feature_029` by `feature_001` interaction is positive.
The negative association between numeric `feature_029` and `pfs_months` is attenuated among patients with `feature_015=1`; the `feature_029` by `feature_015` interaction is positive.

analyses.method: linear regression with continuous-binary interaction and stratified slopes
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_029 * feature_001` interaction coefficient was +0.00735 months per unit (z=5.74, p=9.70e-9). Slopes were -0.00836 for `feature_001=0` and -0.00101 for `feature_001=1`.

analyses.method: linear regression with continuous-binary interaction and stratified slopes
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_029 * feature_015` interaction coefficient was +0.00750 months per unit (z=5.66, p=1.53e-8). Slopes were -0.00797 for `feature_015=0` and -0.00047 for `feature_015=1`.

iterations.update: The `feature_029` adverse slope was concentrated outside the `feature_001=1` and `feature_015=1` subgroups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between numeric `feature_041` and `pfs_months` is weaker in patients with `feature_036=1`; the `feature_041` by `feature_036` interaction is negative.

analyses.method: linear regression with continuous-binary interaction and stratified slopes
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_041 * feature_036` interaction coefficient was -0.00712 months per `feature_041` unit (z=-6.01, p=1.89e-9). Slopes were +0.1804 for `feature_036=0` and +0.1733 for `feature_036=1`.

iterations.update: `feature_041` remained favorable in both `feature_036` strata, but the slope was modestly attenuated for `feature_036=1`.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between numeric `feature_041` and `pfs_months` is weaker at higher `feature_025`; the `feature_041` by `feature_025` interaction is negative.

analyses.method: linear regression with continuous-continuous interaction and stratified slopes
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_041 * feature_025` interaction coefficient was -0.00720 months per product unit (z=-8.81, p<1e-300). Stratified `feature_041` slopes were +0.1800 at `feature_025=0`, +0.1774 at `feature_025=1`, and +0.1631 at `feature_025=2`.

iterations.update: The adverse `feature_025` state reduced, but did not remove, the favorable `feature_041` slope.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The adverse association of higher `feature_025` with `pfs_months` is less negative among patients with `feature_036=1`; the `feature_025` by `feature_036` interaction is positive.

analyses.result_summary: The `feature_025 * feature_036` interaction coefficient was +0.06335 months (z=2.34, p=0.0191). This was statistically significant at 0.05 but small relative to the main effects and weak in the broader interaction screen.

iterations.update: This interaction was treated as tentative because its magnitude was small and its p-value was modest compared with the dominant signals.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between numeric `feature_041` and `pfs_months` is weaker as `feature_056` increases; the `feature_041` by `feature_056` interaction is negative.
The positive association between numeric `feature_041` and `pfs_months` is stronger as `feature_050` increases; the `feature_041` by `feature_050` interaction is positive.

analyses.result_summary: The `feature_041 * feature_056` interaction coefficient was -0.000553 months per product unit (z=-3.29, p=0.00101), a very small but statistically significant attenuation.
The `feature_041 * feature_050` interaction coefficient was +0.002884 months per product unit (z=2.21, p=0.0271), a small borderline-positive interaction.

iterations.update: Continuous-continuous interactions existed but were much smaller than the main effects and the `feature_043` subgroup interactions.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for all other features, `feature_052=1` is associated with lower `pfs_months` than `feature_052=0`.
After adjustment for all other features, `feature_049=1` is associated with lower `pfs_months` than `feature_049=0`.
After adjustment for all other features, `feature_017=1` is associated with higher `pfs_months` than `feature_017=0`.

analyses.result_summary: Full 73-feature linear model coefficient for `feature_052=1` was -0.0265 months (SE=0.00860, z=-3.08, p=0.00206).
Full 73-feature model coefficient for `feature_049=1` was -0.0446 months (SE=0.01964, z=-2.27, p=0.0231).
Full 73-feature model coefficient for `feature_017=1` was +0.0489 months (SE=0.02254, z=2.17, p=0.0301).

iterations.update: A few minor residual associations appeared in the full model, but their absolute effects were below 0.05 months and were not emphasized as clinically meaningful.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for all other features, `feature_037=1` is associated with higher `pfs_months` than `feature_037=0`.
After adjustment for all other features, `feature_018=1` is associated with lower `pfs_months` than `feature_018=0`.

analyses.result_summary: Full 73-feature model coefficient for `feature_037=1` was +0.0104 months and was not significant (SE=0.00776, z=1.34, p=0.179).
Full 73-feature model coefficient for `feature_018=1` was +0.000112 months, opposite the hypothesized direction and not significant (p=0.992).

iterations.update: Several common binary features carried little independent information once the dominant covariates were included.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_061=1` have higher mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_061=0`.
Patients with `feature_048=1` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_048=0`.
After adjustment for all other features, `feature_005=1` is associated with lower `pfs_months` than `feature_005=0`.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate mean comparison for `feature_061=1` versus 0 estimated +0.00076 months with no evidence of association (z=0.02, p=0.982).
Univariate mean comparison for `feature_048=1` versus 0 estimated -0.00253 months with no evidence of association (z=-0.10, p=0.924).
Full 73-feature model coefficient for `feature_005=1` was -0.00137 months and was not significant (p=0.938).

iterations.update: By the final iteration, additional probes mainly found null or negligible effects; the analysis stopped at the 25-iteration cap.
